page: Amazon Web Services c5.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 36 vCPUs and 18 physical cores with hyper-threading, powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The instance is equipped with 72.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the c5.9xlarge in the average tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1426.59 and a multi-core score of 33337.29. In cryptographic and vector operations, the CPU utilizes AVX and AVX-512F instructions, scoring 33816.97 in the PassMark CPU extended instructions test and 15278.36 in the encryption test. Memory performance includes a read-only large block bandwidth of 104.53 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 45.25 GB/sec.

The instance exhibits strong multi-threaded capabilities, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 10571.0 and a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression rate of 66.63 GB/sec. However, the lack of bundled local storage and a relatively low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core represent key architectural tradeoffs, making it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or massive local caching. It is highly optimized for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and high-throughput Redis SET operations.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 36 vCPUs and 18 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 72.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show average performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 30341.37 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 10571.0. It supports AVX and AVX-512F instruction sets, making it highly effective for compute-heavy tasks. This hardware profile is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and high-throughput Redis operations, though the lack of local storage and moderate memory density are notable tradeoffs for data-heavy applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 36 vCPUs, 72 GB DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.4 GHz CPU. It is designed for compute-heavy workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5.9xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 36 vCPUs, 72 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon CPU on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server with 36 dedicated vCPUs, 72 GB DDR4 RAM, and high-frequency Intel Xeon processing for compute-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 36 vCPUs and 18 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor at 3.4 GHz
72.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz with a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
No local storage or GPU hardware, utilizing network-attached storage
12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving
